div1.entryFree:
Martyr, vb. to torture, to torment, to destroy in a savage manner: that face which underneath thy black all-hiding cloak immodestly lies --ed with disgrace, Lucr. 802. speak, gentle sister, who hath --ed thee? Tit. III, 1, 81. Tit. III, 1, 81 hark, wretches, how I mean to m. you, V, 2, 181. despised, distressed, hated, --ed, killed, Rom. IV, 5, 59.
--ed, adjectively, == pertaining to a state of cruel suffering: I can interpret all her --ed signs, Tit. III, 2, 36.
